title: Balloon-Borne Profiling of Global UV-A Attenuation over the Kota Region, Rajasthan, India: Effective Optical Depth, Satellite Aerosol Context, and Instrument Performance from a Low-Cost High-Altitude Flight (paper, code, and data)
description: Manuscript, analysis code, and data for the Akāsha Jyoti high-altitude balloon flight launched from Sultanpur, Kota district, Rajasthan, India (25.298N, 76.192E) on 5 October 2025, reaching ~17.6 km pressure altitude. The primary instrument was a factory-calibrated Apogee SU-202 UV-A radiometer (305-390 nm). Headline result: the effective vertical optical depth of global (hemispherical) UV-A was 0.233 +/- 0.023, versus a direct-beam extinction expectation of 1.74 +/- 0.21 (Rayleigh + MAIAC AOD extrapolated to 360 nm), giving eta = 0.134 +/- 0.021 - a quantified measure of diffuse replenishment under a moderately turbid, non-absorbing atmosphere. The archive contains the SSRN-ready PDF, LaTeX source, all figures, reproduction scripts (Python), the cleaned and raw flight datalogs, and along-track extracts of MAIAC MCD19A2 AOD, Sentinel-5P TROPOMI columns, ERA5 reanalysis, CPCB surface air quality, and NASA POWER surface UV-A used for cross-checks.
